#+title: Financial Products Markup Language
#+description: FpML — the ISDA-managed XML standard for representing OTC derivative transactions. External reference for docs that touch the wire format.
#+type: knowledge
#+level: cross
#+filetags: :knowledge:external:fpml:domain:
#+created: 2024-06-15
#+updated: 2026-05-21

[[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FpML][FpML]] (Financial Products Markup Language) is the ISDA-managed XML
standard for representing over-the-counter (OTC) derivative
transactions. ORE Studio touches FpML mainly through [[id:1A95DFA1-932C-B094-4653-BFCAD266C2AD][Coding
schemes]] — the FpML mechanism for constraining fields to a known set
of values. Return to [[id:CF2C4F3A-9C5C-4E7A-9E16-2A3A8C1D4C11][External knowledge]] / [[id:7474935C-95D1-3034-B833-83DCD9FB3A09][Knowledge]].

* What it is

From [[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FpML][Wikipedia]]:

#+begin_quote
FpML (Financial products Markup Language) is a business information
exchange standard based on Extensible Markup Language (XML) that
enables business-to-business over-the-counter (OTC) financial
derivative transactions online by following W3C standards.

The standard is managed by the International Swaps and Derivatives
Association (ISDA) on behalf of a community of investment banks
that make up the OTC derivatives industry. All categories of
privately negotiated derivatives will eventually be included within
the standard.

FpML is distinct from similar financial standards such as SWIFT
and FIX because it does not specify a network or transport
mechanism.
#+end_quote

* Where it lives

- /Standard home/ — [[https://www.fpml.org/][fpml.org]], maintained by ISDA.
- /In this repo/ — vendored under [[proj:external/fpml][=external/fpml/=]]: the version 5.13
  XSD schemas, split by view ([[proj:external/fpml/5.13/confirmation][=confirmation/=]], [[proj:external/fpml/5.13/reporting][=reporting/=]],
  [[proj:external/fpml/5.13/legal][=legal/=]], [[proj:external/fpml/5.13/recordkeeping][=recordkeeping/=]], [[proj:external/fpml/5.13/pretrade][=pretrade/=]],
  [[proj:external/fpml/5.13/transparency][=transparency/=]] — each with its own =business-processes/=,
  =products/=, and =validation/= subtrees plus a =changes.pdf=), and
  the [[proj:external/fpml/codelist][=codelist/=]] directory of OASIS Genericode XML code lists (the
  coding schemes, business centre scheme, and other enumerations
  reference data is generated from). This is a /reference copy/ our
  codegen pipeline reads from, not a fork. [[proj:external/fpml/methodology.txt][=methodology.txt=]]
  documents how the vendored copy is refreshed and how the FpML
  reference-data SQL is regenerated from it.

* See also

- [[id:1A95DFA1-932C-B094-4653-BFCAD266C2AD][Coding schemes]] — the FpML mechanism we use most directly.
- [[https://www.fpml.org/][fpml.org]] — standard home page.
